title: IRINN Portal

images.alt: IRINN Self Help Portal login — email, PAN, membership ID, or ASN
Applicant dashboard — profile, resources, applications, and wallet
WHOIS updates — APNIC Registry object management (view, create, update)

overview.description: Multi-role Laravel portal for Internet resource registration, KYC verification, APNIC integration, billing, and grievance management.
overview.details:
Enterprise portal for IRINN (Indian Registry for Internet Names and Numbers) operations — supporting members through registration, document upload, multi-stage approval, and resource allocation, while equipping helpdesk, hostmaster, and billing teams with role-based tooling.

The system integrates APNIC Registry APIs for WHOIS and route management, PayU and an internal wallet for payments, IDfy for KYC validation, and E-Invoice compliance — with legacy data migrated from previous IRINN portal and billing systems.

features: Designed and implemented a multi-role portal (User, Admin, Super Admin) with OTP-based authentication and role-based access for helpdesk, hostmaster, and billing teams
Built end-to-end IRINN application workflow — registration, document upload, KYC verification, multi-stage approval (helpdesk → hostmaster → billing), and resource allocation
Integrated APNIC Registry API for WHOIS object management (create/update/delete) and member route registration with async task polling and audit logging
Developed a billing & invoicing module — annual/one-time/reactivation fees, invoice PDF generation (DomPDF), credit notes, TDS handling, and legacy billing data migration
Integrated PayU payment gateway and an internal wallet system for application fees and invoice payments, including guest invoice payment flow
Implemented KYC/identity verification using IDfy APIs for GST, PAN, Udyam, MCA, and ROC/IEC validation during onboarding
Built a grievance/ticketing system with categories, auto-assignment, escalation rules, and admin workflow management
Migrated data from legacy IRINN portal and billing systems via custom import/migration services and console commands
